experiment_card.research_question: Can a grounded long-context contract preserve broader judgment abstraction while requiring line-range source support for every source-linked claim?
experiment_card.hypothesis: On the same selected second-source excerpt and text_judgment_v0 benchmark used by chunked_source_grounding pilots 004 through 006, a one-pass long-context contract that adds judgment principle proposals on top of the line-range-first locator discipline may recover the broader abstraction that the narrowed chunked contracts traded away, without losing line-range locator quality.
experiment_card.evaluation_plan: Validate this proposed ExperimentCard and MethodCard against protocol schemas. After a separately authorized live run, create proposal-only export records and evaluate whether the grounded long-context contract produces judgment principle proposals that preserve conditional logic and cite source-linked claims, source-linked claims with reviewable line-range locator candidates, honest exact/approximate/broad/missing labels, unsupported-claim handling, and metadata-safe locally computed offsets and quote hashes only after line-range validation. The review must preserve comparison value against long_context_judgment_live_pilot_001 on abstraction quality and against chunked_source_grounding_live_pilot_006 on locator quality. Method quality, trading validity, product readiness, strategy evidence, graduation, and architecture are not evaluated by this planning packet.
